preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: SaaS companies needing a modern, ai-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is a modern, AI-ready design system built for SaaS companies. Frames the problem space and qualifies the visitor, critical for consideration-stage organic traffic that needs to quickly confirm relevance.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Once context is set, articulate core capabilities and outcome promises — intent-aware components, AI-readiness, design-to-dev velocity. Validators need to understand what they're evaluating before they'll engage with proof, so this precedes credibility and evidence.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators are skeptical by nature and require third-party validation. Placing trust signals — customer logos, partner endorsements, industry recognition — after the value proposition gives those signals something concrete to validate, building confidence in the claims just made.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: The Validator archetype demands hard proof before any commitment. Metrics, case study data, before/after benchmarks (e.g., '47% faster design-to-production') convert interest into conviction. Positioned after credibility so social proof primes the audience to trust the numbers.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: At the consideration stage, prospects are actively comparing options. A clear pricing and plan comparison frame — showing tiers, what's included, and who each plan is for — removes ambiguity and moves the Validator from evaluation to choice, directly preceding the conversion prompt.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. By this point the Validator has seen value, trust signals, hard evidence, and pricing — the full proof stack. The CTA can now ask for a paid commitment with minimal friction, supported by all preceding justification.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat captures anyone not ready to convert: secondary paths like a free trial, documentation, demo booking, or newsletter. Ensures no visitor leaves without a next step, and provides navigational footer elements for continued exploration.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is a modern, AI-ready design system built for SaaS companies. Frames the problem space and qualifies the visitor, critical for consideration-stage organic traffic that needs to quickly confirm relevance.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Once context is set, articulate core capabilities and outcome promises — intent-aware components, AI-readiness, design-to-dev velocity. Validators need to understand what they're evaluating before they'll engage with proof, so this precedes credibility and evidence.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators are skeptical by nature and require third-party validation. Placing trust signals — customer logos, partner endorsements, industry recognition — after the value proposition gives those signals something concrete to validate, building confidence in the claims just made.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: The Validator archetype demands hard proof before any commitment. Metrics, case study data, before/after benchmarks (e.g., '47% faster design-to-production') convert interest into conviction. Positioned after credibility so social proof primes the audience to trust the numbers.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Consideration stage demands conversion focus. Notes state 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' This aligns with Validator moving toward paid decision.

Validator archetype requires trust signals at page open. Notes specify 'Validator variant always includes trust_signal' and 'with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster,' establishing credibility before the conversion argument.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic intelligence layer above your component library. Components that resolve the right behavior, copy register, and visual emphasis — for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from outcome-oriented descriptions. Notes state 'Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements' and '3-col-icon is the default' for structured feature articulation.

slots.headline: What MODE resolves — and how
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that turn a static component library into a system with judgment.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — so the right experience ships by default, not by exception.

features.title: IA planner with multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces a full page architecture plus Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Powered by Claude Opus. Brief to build in seconds.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage — no manual assignment. Accent weight and hierarchy accumulate semantically toward conversion.

features.title: Behavioral tokens
features.description: Copy density, evidence requirements, and CTA rules are driven by archetype token — not developer judgment. The system encodes the decision, not the person.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Built on Radix UI primitives — zero new APIs to learn.

features.title: Ships as a code kit
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard. No runtime dependency, no lock-in. Configure once, run in under an hour, fork and extend freely.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires attributed credibility at decision moments. Notes specify 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility' and 'with-company-logo for enterprise social proof. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.'

slots.quote: We'd built a solid component library, but every new campaign meant another round of manual variants and Figma handoff. MODE gave the system the context it was missing — now the right component configuration is just the default output.

page.reasoning: Validator stage demands sourced proof. Notes state 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust,' making with-source essential for consideration-stage credibility.

slots.headline: What teams measure after deploying MODE

stats.label: Median time from install to first AI-generated page architecture
Variant output per brief — Mover, Validator, and Explorer generated in a single pass
Ongoing subscription fees — one purchase, lifetime access, no usage-based billing

page.reasoning: Consideration stage benefits from conversion recovery. Notes specify 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows,' directly supporting the paid conversion goal.
